analysis_result: The mean transwell migration cell count for ipNF06.2A-gPTCH1 cells was 82.83 (SD=7.00), compared to 35.67 (SD=6.05) for ipNF06.2A-gGFP control cells. This represents an approximately 2.32-fold increase in migration with PTCH1 knockout (two-sample t-test: t=-8.83, p=0.00091; n=3 replicates per group).
denominator_used: N=3 biological/technical replicates per group (3 values for ipNF06.2A-gGFP and 3 values for ipNF06.2A-gPTCH1) from Fig 5i sheet of MOESM10 source data.
assumptions_made: Assumed the three numeric rows in the Fig 5i sheet represent independent replicates; assumed standard deviation uses sample SD (ddof=1); used two-sample independent t-test (equal variance) as standard for comparing two groups of migration counts; values assumed to be transwell migration cell counts as indicated by the column header.
step_by_step_analysis:
A. DATA LOADED
   - File: 41467_2023_38432_MOESM10_ESM.xlsx, sheet 'Fig 5i'
   - Dimensions: A1:B5 (5 rows, 2 columns)
   - Row 1 (header): 'transwell migration cell count'
   - Row 2 (group labels): 'ipNF06.2A-gGFP' | 'ipNF06.2A-gPTCH1'
   - Rows 3-5 (data): 3 replicate values per group

B. COHORT DEFINITION
   - Two groups: ipNF06.2A-gGFP (control) and ipNF06.2A-gPTCH1 (PTCH1 knockout)
   - 3 replicates per group (n=3 per group)

C. VARIABLE CODING
   - Outcome: transwell migration cell count (continuous, numeric)
   - ipNF06.2A-gGFP values: 41.07, 29.13, 36.82
   - ipNF06.2A-gPTCH1 values: 87.72, 85.95, 74.81

D. COMPUTATION
   - Mean and sample SD (ddof=1) computed via numpy
   - Two-sample independent t-test via scipy.stats.ttest_ind
   - Fold change = mean(gPTCH1) / mean(gGFP)

E. RESULT
   - ipNF06.2A-gGFP control: mean = 35.67, SD = 6.05 (n=3)
   - ipNF06.2A-gPTCH1: mean = 82.83, SD = 7.00 (n=3)
   - Fold change: 2.32x higher migration with PTCH1 knockout
   - Two-sample t-test: t = -8.83, p = 0.00091
   - PTCH1 knockout significantly increased transwell migration

F. CAVEATS
   - Very small sample size (n=3 per group); t-test has limited power and assumes normality
   - Used independent t-test (equal variance); Welch's t-test or Mann-Whitney would yield similar conclusions given clear separation
   - Replicate type (biological vs technical) not specified in source data
